page: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 192 dedicated physical cores of the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture running at 2.7 GHz. The system features 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via 11400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with uncached read speeds reaching 895.65 GB/sec and write speeds at 412.94 GB/sec. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the average category.

The server demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, achieving top-tier results in PassMark database operations and Redis SET operations at 374.08 million operations per second. While prompt processing for large language models is strong, actual text generation speeds are average to weak, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive databases, large-scale data compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server utilizing 192 dedicated physical cores of the 2.7 GHz AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and Redis SET operations. However, single-core processing and LLM text generation speeds remain average to weak due to the lack of hardware accelerators. This resource-dense configuration is highly efficient for workloads requiring massive memory capacity and fast local storage, such as large-scale databases, multi-threaded data processing, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 Graviton ARMv9 cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11400 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8gd.metal-48xl features 192 Graviton cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 11400 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal ARMv9 server combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with high-speed local NVMe storage for intensive database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 physical cores at 2.7 GHz.
1536 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 11400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory read/write bandwidth.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer.
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis deployments, and software compilation.
